title: RADIUS Accounting Discrepancies
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="radius:accounting"
| eval acct_status=case(
  match(_raw,"Acct-Status-Type.*Start"), "Start",
  match(_raw,"Acct-Status-Type.*Stop"), "Stop",
  match(_raw,"Acct-Status-Type.*Interim"), "Interim",
  1=1, "Unknown")
| rex field=_raw "Acct-Session-Id=(?<session_id>[^\s]+)"
| rex field=_raw "User-Name=(?<user>[^\s]+)"
| rex field=_raw "NAS-IP-Address=(?<nas>[^\s]+)"
| where acct_status!="Unknown" AND acct_status!="Interim"
| stats count(eval(acct_status="Start")) as starts, count(eval(acct_status="Stop")) as stops by session_id, user, nas
| eval discrepancy=abs(starts - stops)
| where discrepancy > 0
| sort -discrepancy
description: Start/stop mismatches indicating dropped sessions or potential abuse. Accounting discrepancies can hide unauthorized access, session hijacking, or billing/audit gaps.
value: Start/stop mismatches indicating dropped sessions or potential abuse. Accounting discrepancies can hide unauthorized access, session hijacking, or billing/audit gaps.
grandmaExplanation: Start/stop mismatches indicating dropped sessions or potential abuse ‚Äî so unsafe traffic and policy bypasses are caught quickly.
implementation: Ingest RADIUS accounting logs with Acct-Session-Id, Acct-Status-Type, User-Name, NAS-IP-Address. Build session state table: each Start should have exactly one Stop. Alert on sessions with Start but no Stop (orphaned sessions) or Stop without Start (potential replay). Report on discrepancy rate and top NAS/users. Use Interim-Update to validate long-running sessions.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-ise`, `Splunk_TA_windows` (NPS), FreeRADIUS syslog, RADIUS accounting TA.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: RADIUS accounting records (Acct-Status-Type Start/Stop/Interim-Update).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Ingest RADIUS accounting logs with Acct-Session-Id, Acct-Status-Type, User-Name, NAS-IP-Address. Build session state table: each Start should have exactly one Stop. Alert on sessions with Start but no Stop (orphaned sessions) or Stop without Start (potential replay). Report on discrepancy rate and top NAS/users. Use Interim-Update to validate long-running sessions.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="radius:accounting"
| eval acct_status=case(
  match(_raw,"Acct-Status-Type.*Start"), "Start",
  match(_raw,"Acct-Status-Type.*Stop"), "Stop",
  match(_raw,"Acct-Status-Type.*Interim"), "Interim",
  1=1, "Unknown")
| rex field=_raw "Acct-Session-Id=(?<session_id>[^\s]+)"
| rex field=_raw "User-Name=(?<user>[^\s]+)"
| rex field=_raw "NAS-IP-Address=(?<nas>[^\s]+)"
| where acct_status!="Unknown" AND acct_status!="Interim"
| stats count(eval(acct_status="Start")) as starts, count(eval(acct_status="Stop")) as stops by session_id, user, nas
| eval discrepancy=abs(starts - stops)
| where discrepancy > 0
| sort -discrepancy
```

Understanding this SPL

**RADIUS Accounting Discrepancies** ‚Äî Start/stop mismatches indicating dropped sessions or potential abuse. Accounting discrepancies can hide unauthorized access, session hijacking, or billing/audit gaps.

Documented **Data sources**: RADIUS accounting records (Acct-Status-Type Start/Stop/Interim-Update). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_cisco-ise`, `Splunk_TA_windows` (NPS), FreeRADIUS syslog, RADIUS accounting TA. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: nac; **sourcetype**: radius:accounting. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=nac, sourcetype="radius:accounting". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **acct_status** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Extracts fields with `rex` (regular expression).
‚Ä¢ Extracts fields with `rex` (regular expression).
‚Ä¢ Extracts fields with `rex` (regular expression).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where acct_status!="Unknown" AND acct_status!="Interim"` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by session_id, user, nas** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **discrepancy** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where discrepancy > 0` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (sessions with discrepancies), Bar chart (discrepancy count by NAS), Single value (orphaned sessions), Line chart (discrepancy trend).
visualization: Table (sessions with discrepancies), Bar chart (discrepancy count by NAS), Single value (orphaned sessions), Line chart (discrepancy trend).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
